beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light stretching at home.
beats.reasoning: Fresh Monday morning with full energy; Maren starts with her own yoga routine before heading out into a sunny day.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila yoga studio, taking scenic route through Magnolia.
beats.reasoning: Maren always takes the scenic route; avoiding Light Rail closure (9am–3pm) between Westlake and U-District by driving, and the Magnolia detour fits her personality.

beats.activity: Lead early-morning vinyasa and wellness coaching session.; Second teaching slot: restorative yoga and one-on-one coaching.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning class for working professionals; Maren is energized and focused at the start of the week.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab healthy bowl and tea at studio café.
beats.reasoning: USA vs Wales match airs at 11am, but Maren opts for a quiet lunch at the studio rather than joining downtown bar crowds—fits her preference for close friends over big crowds.

beats.activity: Afternoon teaching: power yoga and wellness consultation.
beats.reasoning: Maren continues her teaching block mid-day; the sunny weather and full-energy mood keep her engaged with clients.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle, taking scenic route through Green River valley.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon commute home; Maren again chooses a scenic drive over direct route, enjoying the sunny weather and avoiding rush-hour congestion.

beats.activity: Stop at neighborhood market for fresh groceries and errands.
beats.reasoning: Quick errand on the way home; sunny weather makes the short walk pleasant, and Maren prefers local shopping to big-box stores.

beats.activity: Home knitting project and poetry prep for tonight's open-mic.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon wind-down with her favorite hobbies; Maren uses the sunny day indoors to work on knitting and refine her open-mic poems for later.

beats.activity: Meet close friend for coffee and catch-up before open-mic.
beats.reasoning: Maren values time with close friends; a quick coffee date before the evening open-mic fits her social preferences and energizes her for performance.

beats.activity: Attend open-mic poetry night and perform.
beats.reasoning: Monday evening poetry and open-mic; Maren's competitive edge and creative outlet shine in this intimate venue with familiar faces rather than large crowds.

beats.activity: Light dinner and wind-down at home; journal and reflect on the day.
beats.reasoning: Return home energized from poetry night; Maren prepares for bed with a light meal and reflection, maintaining her wellness routine.

diary:
Woke early to that perfect June light streaming through the bedroom. The drive to Tukwila through Magnolia was exactly what I needed—water glinting between the trees, my mind already settling into teaching mode. Led three solid classes today; there's something about Monday mornings that brings people in ready to work. Had a good conversation with one of my regulars about her shoulder tension during the afternoon session.

The drive back felt shorter, maybe because I was already thinking about tonight. Grabbed some greens and fresh herbs at Whole Foods, then spent the afternoon knitting and scribbling down some new lines for the open-mic. Met Sarah at Espresso Vivace—we haven't caught up properly in weeks, and it felt good to just sit and talk without rushing. The poetry night was energizing; my piece landed well, and hearing other voices in that intimate space at the Arts Center reminded me why I keep showing up. Home now with tea, feeling that good kind of tired.
thought_process: I made a conscious choice to take the scenic routes today even though the Light Rail was down—partly practical, partly intentional. I needed the drive time to transition between roles. The World Cup buzz downtown didn't really pull at me; I'm not much of a sports person, and I was already committed to the studio schedule. My mood shifted around mid-afternoon when I started preparing for the open-mic—that creative anticipation kicked in. Teaching all morning kept me grounded and present, but there's a different energy when I'm performing my own work. I think I needed both today: the structured, service-oriented morning and then the vulnerable, expressive evening. The coffee with Sarah was the hinge point—it reminded me to show up fully, whether in a yoga studio or on stage.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
